deep_dive.title: Synergizing Global Pattern Learning and Time Order Characterization in Mobile Channel Prediction: An RWKV-Based Approach
deep_dive.abstract: Owing to the potential to reduce pilot overhead and mitigate channel aging, channel prediction is emerging as an important research topic in wireless communications. Meanwhile, deep neural networks are becoming a foundational technology for high-precision prediction thanks to their excellent non-linear representation capabilities. In this paper, we conceive a task-driven prediction network, which aims to deeply synergize the following two functions: learning global patterns for shareable features across adjacent time slots and structurally encoding time order to characterize the inherent causality within the channel dynamics. To implement channel prediction accuracy, we employ RWKV (receptance weighted key value) as network backbone and adapt it to the task's specific characteristics, utilizing its deep interleaved learning architecture to extract global patterns across multiple channel samples and leveraging its unique exponential decay to characterize temporal order. These task-driven unique designs significantly improve the learning efficiency of prediction network. Comprehensive experimental evaluations demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method over current data-driven methods, such as long short-term memory and Transformer, in the channel prediction task, including 1.84~4.29 dB gains in normalized mean squared error and 2.6~10.5 percentage point gains in cosine correlation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: mobile channel prediction using RWKV neural network
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用RWKV神经网络进行移动信道预测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用RWKV神经网络进行移动信道预测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用RWKV神经网络进行移动信道预测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Applies RWKV, a novel neural architecture, to wireless channel prediction, synergizing global pattern learning and time order characterization for high-accuracy mobile channel prediction.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对无线通信中的信道预测问题，提出一种基于RWKV的任务驱动预测网络，该网络深度融合了全局模式学习（用于相邻时隙的共享特征）和时序编码（用于刻画信道动态的因果性）。实验表明，该方法能够有效降低导频开销并缓解信道老化，为高效信道建模提供了新的神经网络架构思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对无线通信中的信道预测问题，提出一种基于RWKV的任务驱动预测网络，该网络深度融合了全局模式学习（用于相邻时隙的共享特征）和时序编码（用于刻画信道动态的因果性）。实验表明，该方法能够有效降低导频开销并缓解信道老化，为高效信道建模提供了新的神经网络架构思路。

quick_skim.title: Context-Aware Markov VAE for CSI Compression in Wireless Systems
quick_skim.abstract: This paper considers neural channel state information (CSI) compression for time-varying massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) channels in frequency division duplex (FDD) systems with limited feedback resources. The main challenge lies in obtaining a compact and efficient representation of the CSI given that it exhibits strong temporal correlation across successive snapshots. Existing memoryless compression models do not exploit this property, while simple temporal extensions often incorporate multiple observations without explicitly modeling the latent dynamics. We propose a context-aware compression framework based on a k-memory Markov variational autoencoder (k-MMVAE), which uses a finite temporal window to capture the evolution of CSI in the latent space. The model introduces Markov-structured latent dynamics with finite memory, enabling efficient use of temporal dependencies for compression. Simulation results show that the proposed approach improves target CSI reconstruction performance compared to memoryless and weakly sequential baselines, particularly at low and moderate compression rates. These results suggest that explicit latent temporal modeling can provide an effective mechanism for CSI compression under limited feedback constraints.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Neural CSI compression for wireless MIMO channels
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于无线MIMO信道的神经CSI压缩
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于无线MIMO信道的神经CSI压缩
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于无线MIMO信道的神经CSI压缩
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a context-aware Markov VAE for CSI compression in massive MIMO, exploiting temporal correlation with a finite-memory latent model.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对时变大规模MIMO系统中的CSI压缩问题，本文提出基于k-记忆马尔可夫变分自编码器（k-MMVAE）的框架。该框架通过有限时间窗口建模CSI的时序演化，克服了无记忆压缩的不足。在FDD反馈受限场景下，所提方法实现了比传统方法更高的压缩效率和重构精度，适用于通信信道建模与反馈优化。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对时变大规模MIMO系统中的CSI压缩问题，本文提出基于k-记忆马尔可夫变分自编码器（k-MMVAE）的框架。该框架通过有限时间窗口建模CSI的时序演化，克服了无记忆压缩的不足。在FDD反馈受限场景下，所提方法实现了比传统方法更高的压缩效率和重构精度，适用于通信信道建模与反馈优化。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: neural networks for communication channel modeling

quick_skim.title: Connections Between Pairs of Filters Improve the Accuracy of Convolutional Neural Networks
quick_skim.abstract: While researchers continue to find new and improved network structures for CNNs, most of the newly invented architectures still rely on the traditional pattern of stacking convolutional blocks and separating them with pointwise activation functions. However, there are drawbacks to a network purely building on pointwise nonlinearities. One alternative is to introduce a pairwise connection between two filters of a network. Typical connection functions use multiplications or the minimum operation to realize logical AND connections. In this paper, we go one step further by demonstrating that CNNs can benefit from more general connections, which include parameters that are learned. With such parameters, the network is able to implement different connections in different network layers and better adapt the connection function to the task at hand.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Novel CNN architecture with pairwise filter connections
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 具有成对滤波器连接的新型CNN架构
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 具有成对滤波器连接的新型CNN架构
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 具有成对滤波器连接的新型CNN架构
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper introduces learnable pairwise connections between CNN filters, improving accuracy over traditional pointwise-nonlinearity-only architectures.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出一种改进CNN架构的方法，通过在滤波器对之间引入可学习的连接（如参数化乘法或最小值操作），替代传统逐点激活函数。该方法在多个图像分类数据集上提升了准确率，且参数量增加很小。这种新型连接范式可迁移至通信领域的神经网络设计，如用于信道建模或均衡的卷积结构。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出一种改进CNN架构的方法，通过在滤波器对之间引入可学习的连接（如参数化乘法或最小值操作），替代传统逐点激活函数。该方法在多个图像分类数据集上提升了准确率，且参数量增加很小。这种新型连接范式可迁移至通信领域的神经网络设计，如用于信道建模或均衡的卷积结构。

quick_skim.title: Squeeze-Release: Iterative Pruning with Exact Structural Minimization
quick_skim.abstract: Unstructured pruning produces sparse weight tensors, but the standard implementation keeps tensor shapes unchanged so the deployed model is no smaller than before pruning. We present an exact structural rewrite, which we call minimization, that converts a masked network into a smaller dense network with the same forward function up to floating-point rounding. The Squeeze-Release cycle iterates pruning and minimization with an intermediate release step that re-enables the exact-zero positions inside the compacted tensors as small calibrated noise, turning otherwise wasted capacity back into trainable parameters. Successive cycles use that capacity to find structural redundancy a single pass cannot reach. We additionally introduce CompensatedLayerNorm, a function-preserving replacement for LayerNorm that extends minimization to channel reduction across LayerNorm-equipped residual streams. Squeeze-Release compresses the deployable network to 39x smaller than the unpruned model on a fully-connected model network and 14.8x smaller on modern CNN (ConvNeXt-Tiny), at comparable accuracy. In addition we prove that the rewrite can be extended to transformer architectures.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: iterative pruning method can compress neural networks for communication efficiency
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 迭代剪枝方法可用于压缩通信中的神经网络，提高效率
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 迭代剪枝方法可用于压缩通信中的神经网络，提高效率
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 迭代剪枝方法可用于压缩通信中的神经网络，提高效率
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a Squeeze-Release cycle with exact structural minimization that converts masked networks into smaller dense ones, enabling efficient deployment.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对剪枝后网络尺寸不变导致能效低的问题，提出了精确结构化最小化方法，将掩码网络转换为更小的稠密网络，并设计了Squeeze-Release循环迭代剪枝与释放，利用中间校准噪声挖掘结构冗余。该方法可有效压缩神经网络，降低计算和存储成本，适用于通信系统中对高效神经网络的需求。实验证明了多次循环能发现更多冗余。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对剪枝后网络尺寸不变导致能效低的问题，提出了精确结构化最小化方法，将掩码网络转换为更小的稠密网络，并设计了Squeeze-Release循环迭代剪枝与释放，利用中间校准噪声挖掘结构冗余。该方法可有效压缩神经网络，降低计算和存储成本，适用于通信系统中对高效神经网络的需求。实验证明了多次循环能发现更多冗余。

quick_skim.title: Explainable Task-Oriented Token Communication for AI-Native 6G Networks
quick_skim.abstract: The integration of Foundation Models (FMs) and wireless communications is driving the evolution of image communication from bit-accurate transmission toward task-oriented transmission. However, existing task-oriented image communication methods still face three major challenges: insufficient task-oriented Token representation, inadequate collaboration between Visual Tokens and Task Tokens, and limited interpretability of task decisions. To address these challenges, we propose an Explainable Task-Oriented Token Communication (ET-TokenCom) framework. By treating Tokens as unified units for information representation and transmission, the proposed framework constructs an end-to-end communication link that spans visual perception, wireless transmission, and task reasoning. At the transmitter, the ET-TokenCom framework extracts Visual Tokens from images to preserve low-level visual information. Meanwhile, Task Tokens generated by the FM are introduced to represent the target information and decision intent required by the current task. A Cross-Modal Attention (CMA) fusion mechanism is further designed, enabling Task Tokens to explicitly guide the selection, weighting, and transmission of Visual Tokens. At the receiver, the framework integrates Token decoding with an explainable output mechanism, where attention heatmaps are generated to highlight critical perceptual regions under different task objectives and reveal the influence of Task Tokens on the outputs. Finally, simulation results validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed ET-TokenCom framework.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: deep learning framework for task-oriented image transmission in 6G
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向6G任务导向图像传输的深度学习框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向6G任务导向图像传输的深度学习框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向6G任务导向图像传输的深度学习框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes ET-TokenCom, an explainable task-oriented token communication framework for AI-native 6G networks, treating tokens as unified units for visual perception, transmission, and tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有任务导向图像通信中token表示不足、视觉token与任务token协作差、可解释性低的问题，提出了可解释任务导向token通信（ET-TokenCom）框架。该框架将token作为统一的信息表示与传输单元，构建了从视觉感知到无线传输再到任务决策的端到端链路。在6G AI原生网络背景下，该方法提升了任务导向传输的效率和可解释性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有任务导向图像通信中token表示不足、视觉token与任务token协作差、可解释性低的问题，提出了可解释任务导向token通信（ET-TokenCom）框架。该框架将token作为统一的信息表示与传输单元，构建了从视觉感知到无线传输再到任务决策的端到端链路。在6G AI原生网络背景下，该方法提升了任务导向传输的效率和可解释性。

quick_skim.title: Scaling Adaptive Depth with Norm-Agnostic Residual Networks
quick_skim.abstract: Residual architectures are ubiquitous in deep learning, but they suffer from a subtle structural limitation: the norm of the residual stream can grow rapidly with depth. As a result, updates from later layers become small relative to the accumulated residual state. This reduces their impact on the representation and limits the benefits of scaling models in depth. To address this, we introduce NAG, a norm-agnostic residual architecture that separates magnitude from directional information in the residual stream, preserving meaningful layer contributions throughout depth and preventing later updates from being systematically suppressed by residual-norm growth. Importantly, NAG introduces only a negligible number of additional parameters and relies on simple operations that are easily kernel-fusible, preserving training efficiency in practice. We show that this architecture outperforms baseline Transformers, with gains that increase substantially as depth grows, enabling effective training of much deeper models. The norm-agnostic formulation also leads to an interpretable Mixture-of-Depths (MoD) mechanism that adaptively skips both attention and MLP layers. Beyond serving as a post-training accuracy-compute tradeoff, this mechanism can be used as a pretraining-time scaling strategy: under iso-FLOP training, compute saved by reducing per-token forward-pass cost can be reinvested into training on more tokens while keeping the total parameter count and KV-cache budget fixed. In our experiments, moderate Mixture-of-Depths rates of approximately 20%-25% match full-depth baseline performance under equal training compute while substantially reducing the number of executed layer parameters and forward-pass FLOPs. These results identify sparsity in depth as a new scaling axis for fixed-compute training, enabling very deep yet FLOP-efficient models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 新型范数不可知残差架构
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 新型范数不可知残差架构
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 新型范数不可知残差架构
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes NAG, a norm-agnostic residual network that separates magnitude from direction, enabling deeper and more effective scaling with negligible overhead.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文发现残差网络中残差流范数随深度增长会抑制后续层贡献。为此提出NAG架构，将残差流的模长与方向解耦，使得深层更新免受范数增长压制。NAG仅增加极少量参数，在深度网络训练中表现出更好的性能。这种架构改进可应用于通信系统中的深度神经网络，如用于均衡或信道估计的深度残差网络。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文发现残差网络中残差流范数随深度增长会抑制后续层贡献。为此提出NAG架构，将残差流的模长与方向解耦，使得深层更新免受范数增长压制。NAG仅增加极少量参数，在深度网络训练中表现出更好的性能。这种架构改进可应用于通信系统中的深度神经网络，如用于均衡或信道估计的深度残差网络。
